---
title: "Shamindra's February 2026 Roundup"
description: |
  A quick roundup of any interesting February 2026 activities
categories: [personal, roundup]
slug: shrotriya2026february26roundup
citation:
  id: shrotriya2026february26roundup
date: "2026-02-28"
image: images/preview-february-2026-01.png
bibliography: ../../refs.bib
draft: true
crossref:
  custom:
    - kind: float
      reference-prefix: Video
      key: vid
format:
  html:
    code-link: true
execute:
  echo: true
editor_options:
  markdown:
    wrap: 80
---

```{=html}
<style>
.quarto-layout-cell img {
  border: 1px solid #000;
}
.quarto-video {
  max-width: 75%;
  margin: 0 auto;
}
.quarto-video iframe {
  border: 1px solid #000;
}
.quarto-float-vid {
  text-align: center;
}
.quarto-layout-panel .quarto-video {
  max-width: 100%;
  margin: 0;
}
.quarto-layout-panel .quarto-float-vid {
  text-align: center;
}
.quarto-layout-panel .quarto-float-vid figcaption {
  font-size: 0.85em;
}
.quarto-layout-panel .quarto-layout-row {
  gap: 0;
}
.quarto-layout-panel blockquote {
  border-left: 4px solid #c0392b;
  padding-left: 1em;
  margin-left: 0;
}
.column-margin .quarto-float-vid {
  width: 350px;
  max-width: none;
}
.column-margin .quarto-video {
  max-width: 100%;
}
</style>
```

## Introduction

Welcome to the February 2026 roundup! Similar to
[last time](../../posts/2020-01-27-shrotriya2020january20roundup/){target="_blank"}
I'm documenting anything interesting I come across and any activities
I get up to. This is more for my personal benefit but may also help
others.

## Summary

<!-- 2-3 line overview of highlights: key skills learned, books read,
     articles discovered, and anything else notable this month. -->

## Interesting Articles

[How Markdown took over the world](https://www.anildash.com/2026/01/09/how-markdown-took-over-the-world/){target="_blank"} by Anil Dash.

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" icon=false}

## Key Takeaways

- We really are in the era of markdown-driven development.

:::

## Interesting Tutorials

### Large Language Models in Five Formulas

:::::: {layout="[40, 60]" layout-valign="top"}
::: {#vid-feb26-llm-five-formulas}
{{< video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KCXDr-UOb9A start="2989" title="Large Language Models in Five Formulas by Sasha Rush" aspect-ratio="4x3" >}}

[LLMs in Five Formulas](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KCXDr-UOb9A&t=2989s){target="_blank"}.
:::

:::: {}
[Large Language Models in Five Formulas](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KCXDr-UOb9A&t=2989s){target="_blank"} by Sasha Rush[^src-llm-five-formulas].

- A truly remarkable lecture distilling Prof. Rush's deep insights
  into the five features of LLMs that make them 'tick'.
::::
::::::

[^src-llm-five-formulas]: There is also a recorded
    [tutorial format](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=k9DnQPrfJQs&t=3571s){target="_blank"}
    of this lecture if you prefer a slightly relaxed pace with
    interaction from the audience.

## Interesting Books

### Audiobooks

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" icon=false}

## Audiobook - Tip

I came to a reading epiphany in early 2026, namely, that *for me* **self-help books should be listened to as audiobooks** rather than read as paperbacks. This simple change has made a world of difference. I previously found the act of reading such books quite tedious and dry, often given their prescriptive tone. Now I can use my morning and evening walks to actively listen to them. Since I have Spotify premium, many books (including those listed below) are included in the membership. In case you feel similarly, this tip might help you sample more of this genre. There is often *some* nugget to take away from each one.

:::

:::::: {layout="[15, 85]" layout-valign="top"}
![](images/feb26-life-changing-magic.jpg){fig-alt="Book cover of The Life-Changing Magic of Tidying Up by Marie Kondo"}

:::: {}
[Life Changing Magic of Tidying Up (audiobook)](https://open.spotify.com/show/4evjgM54KB8lxxT87caPCd){target="_blank"} by Marie Kondo (narrated by Lucy Scott)[^src-life-changing-magic].

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" icon=false}

## Key Takeaways

This is a genuinely remarkable book and one that I'm more a decade late to. I had heard all of the "spark joy" memes when the Netflix show landed, and simply assumed this was another basic decluttering gimmick and ignored it. How wrong I was. This is a very deep book about life prioritization disguised as a home tidying manifesto. I learned many practical skills, but the main three biggest takeaways were as follows. First, Marie Kondo (or 'KonMari') notes that tidying your home should be *one-off* activity, not a recurring one. Second, the book emphasizes the use of qualitative decision making over quantitative for tidying. And finally, maintaining tidyness can be done via principled methods, e.g. the KonMari folding technique. A bonus lesson from the book, is that tidying is the *first* step towards building a better you, not the final one.

:::
::::
::::::

[^src-life-changing-magic]: Image source for
    *The Life-Changing Magic of Tidying Up*:
    [Open Library](https://openlibrary.org/isbn/9781607747307){target="_blank"}.

:::::: {layout="[15, 85]" layout-valign="top"}
![](images/feb26-win-friends.jpg){fig-alt="Book cover of How to Win Friends and Influence People by Dale Carnegie"}

:::: {}
[How to Win Friends and Influence People (audiobook)](https://open.spotify.com/episode/1jDBhhffayv293DtCH4rSP){target="_blank"} by Dale Carnegie[^src-win-friends].

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" icon=false}

## Key Takeaways

- We really are in the era of markdown-driven development.

:::
::::
::::::

[^src-win-friends]: Image source for
    *How to Win Friends and Influence People*:
    [Spotify](https://open.spotify.com/episode/1jDBhhffayv293DtCH4rSP){target="_blank"}.

:::::: {layout="[15, 85]" layout-valign="top"}
![](images/feb26-diary-of-a-ceo.jpg){fig-alt="Book cover of The Diary of a CEO by Steven Bartlett"}

:::: {}
[Diary of a CEO (audiobook)](https://open.spotify.com/show/7iQXmUT7XGuZSzAMjoNWlX){target="_blank"} by Steven Bartlett (narrated by the author)[^src-diary-of-a-ceo].

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" icon=false}

## Key Takeaways

- We really are in the era of markdown-driven development.

:::
::::
::::::

[^src-diary-of-a-ceo]: Image source for *The Diary of a CEO*:
    [Open Library](https://openlibrary.org/works/OL37187091W){target="_blank"}.

## Skills Learned

I really enjoy learning skills in a **principled manner**, so that the most mundane and routine tasks can be approached in a way that makes them approachable, easy, and fun!

Here are some of the cool skills I picked up this month.

### The KonMari Fold

:::::: {layout="[40, 60]" layout-valign="top"}
::: {#vid-feb26-konmari-fold}
{{< video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IjkmqbJTLBM start="7" title="The KonMari Fold by Marie Kondo" aspect-ratio="4x3" >}}

[The KonMari Fold](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IjkmqbJTLBM&t=7s){target="_blank"}.
:::

:::: {}
After becoming a [student of KonMari](#audiobooks), I decided to raise my folding game with her method. It is a game-changer. The The main idea can be summarized in three-steps:

> 1. **Shapes matter.** Rectangular shapes are easiest to fold[^fn-konmari-3d].
> 2. **Rectangulate.** So quickly first fold any item into the largest rectangle.
> 3. **Divide and stand.** Repeatedly fold down the rectangle so that the item stands on its own.

::::
::::::

[^fn-konmari-3d]: Technically we are in 3D land, but we mean
    rectangles looking down 👀.

### Over/Under Roadie Wrap

:::::: {layout="[40, 60]" layout-valign="top"}
::: {#vid-feb26-roadie-wrap}
{{< video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zjpBXx8oNOc start="2" title="Over/Under Roadie Wrap by Rattlesnake Cable Company" aspect-ratio="4x3" >}}

[Over/Under Roadie Wrap](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zjpBXx8oNOc&t=2s){target="_blank"}.
:::

:::: {}
After collecting hundreds of cables and wires, I realized that I'd never learned a proper way to coil them up. The "roadie-wrap" is a well-tested technique used by grips who have to coil (and uncoil!) hundreds of cables daily.

> 1. **Tangle-free.** Uncoiling never leads to tangles.
> 2. **Easy recoiling.** Recoiling is just a matter of nailing the over/under technique.
> 3. **Works small.** You can use this on small cables, and your cables will last a lot longer.
::::
::::::

## Concluding Thoughts
